chief_data.selection_rationale: Candidate 1 wins with a combined score of 0.793, driven by its exceptional chemistry score of 0.970 which reflects optimal conversion at X=0.63 for this challenging SNAr reaction. Runner-up candidate 4 scored 0.778, held back by lower chemistry performance (0.850) due to reduced conversion (X=0.47), while candidate 6 scored 0.772 with similar chemistry limitations. The balanced objective favored candidate 1's superior conversion despite lower productivity, as the 30% design envelope shows robust operability with τ ranging 89-165 min and Q ranging 0.046-0.086 mL/min. The most critical uncertainty is whether the estimated kinetics from batch data accurately predict flow performance, particularly the temperature dependence at 150°C. The first bench experiment should verify steady-state conversion by running the reaction mixture at design conditions (τ=127 min, Q=0.066 mL/min) and sampling at 3τ and 5τ to confirm X=0.63 is achieved.
resolved_tradeoffs: id=1 vs id=4: id=1 has higher chemistry score (0.970 vs 0.850) due to better conversion (X=0.63 vs 0.47), outweighing id=4's geometry advantage
id=1 vs id=6: id=1 has wider design envelope (Q range 0.041 vs 0.180 mL/min) providing better operational flexibility
Design envelope comparison: id=1 has τ range 76.4 min, id=4 has τ range 48.3 min, id=6 has τ range 48.3 min
remaining_uncertainties: Kinetics estimated from batch data; flow enhancement at 150°C in DMF not experimentally verified
Heat transfer effects at high temperature (150°C) in small diameter tubing may affect local conversion
chief_data.experiment_recommendation: Run reaction mixture at design conditions (τ=127 min, Q=0.066 mL/min, T=150°C) and sample at t=3τ and t=5τ to verify X=0.63 conversion is achieved.

pump_flowrates.derivation: ṅ_lim=0.0028 mmol/min × eq=1.0 / C_feed=0.15 M × 0.567 = 0.03297 mL/min
ṅ_lim=0.0028 mmol/min × eq=1.0 / C_feed=0.15 M × 0.567 = 0.03297 mL/min
